questions.question: Who has access to the Audit Log feature in Notion?

options.text: All users on the Business Plan
options.explanation: The Audit Log is not available to all users on the Business Plan; it is an Enterprise Plan feature.

options.text: Organization Owners on the Enterprise Plan
options.explanation: The Audit Log feature is available to organization owners specifically on the Enterprise Plan.

options.text: Workspace Administrators on the Pro Plan
options.explanation: Audit logs are only available to Organization Owners on the Enterprise Plan, not Pro Plan administrators.

options.text: All members of a workspace regardless of plan
options.explanation: Access is restricted to Organization Owners on the Enterprise Plan to ensure security compliance.

questions.question: How do you navigate to the Audit Log in your workspace?

options.text: Open the workspace switcher, select Manage organization, go to Data & Compliance, and select Audit log.
options.explanation: This is the exact path: Workspace switcher > Manage organization > Data & Compliance > Audit log.

options.text: Go to Settings > Security > Audit Log
options.explanation: The Audit Log is found under Data & Compliance, not directly under Settings.

options.text: Select 'Manage Organization' from the top right corner of any page
options.explanation: You must first select 'Manage organization' via the workspace switcher before you can access the Data & Compliance tab.

options.text: Go to the Admin Panel > User Management > Audit Log
options.explanation: The path involves the workspace switcher and the Data & Compliance tab, not an Admin Panel.

questions.question: By default, how are audit log events sorted?

options.text: Chronological order (oldest first)
options.explanation: By default, events are shown in reverse chronological order (newest first).

options.text: Alphabetical order by event type
options.explanation: Events are not sorted alphabetically; they follow a time-based order.

options.text: Reverse chronological order (newest first)
options.explanation: The audit log displays events in reverse chronological order by default.

options.text: Sorted by importance or severity
options.explanation: The sort order is determined by time, not by the severity or importance of the event.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a category of Audit Log events?

options.text: Page events
options.explanation: Page events are a valid category that includes edits, views, and creations.

options.text: Data source events
options.explanation: Data source events are a valid category covering database-related activities.

options.text: Project events
options.explanation: There is no 'Project events' category; the valid categories are Page, Data Source, Teamspace, Workspace, and Account events.

options.text: Account events
options.explanation: Account events are a valid category covering logins, passwords, and profile changes.

questions.question: What information is included in an Audit Log entry by default?

options.text: The full content of the page edited
options.explanation: While the event describes what happened (e.g., 'Page edited'), the full content is not included in the log entry.

options.text: Who took the action and when, and where available, the IP address
options.explanation: Audit logs record the actor, timestamp, and IP address (when available) for each event.

options.text: The email addresses of all collaborators on the page
options.explanation: The log records the specific user who performed the action, not all collaborators.

options.text: The browser type and operating system used
options.explanation: Browser and OS details are not included; only the user, time, and IP address are captured.

questions.question: What does the 'Page permanently deleted' event indicate?

options.text: A user moved a page to Trash but did not empty it
options.explanation: Moving to trash is a separate event. Permanently deleted means the page was removed from Trash.

options.text: A page was removed from Trash by a user or automatically after 30 days
options.explanation: This event occurs when a page is removed from Trash, either manually or automatically by Notion after 30 days.

options.text: A user deleted the page without sending it to Trash first
options.explanation: The 'Page deleted from trash' event occurs after the page is already in the Trash.

options.text: A user restored a page from Trash
options.explanation: Restoration is a separate event type ('Page restored').

questions.question: How can you view related events if an Audit Log entry appears to be part of a larger group?

options.text: Click the 'View Details' button in the sidebar
options.explanation: The method described in the article involves hovering over the row and clicking the magnifying glass icon.

options.text: Hover over the Audit Log row and click the magnifying glass
options.explanation: Hovering over the row and clicking the magnifying glass allows you to filter for related events that were caused by the same action.

options.text: Use the search bar at the top of the page
options.explanation: The search bar is used to find specific information, not to view related events for a specific row.

options.text: Expand the event card by double-clicking the row
options.explanation: Double-clicking is not the method specified for viewing related grouped events.

questions.question: Which Teamspace event type signifies that a user has been removed from the teamspace by an owner?

options.text: Member removed from teamspace
options.explanation: This event type specifically indicates that a teamspace owner has removed a member.

options.text: Member left the teamspace
options.explanation: While this records when a user leaves, 'Member removed from teamspace' specifically implies the action taken by the owner.

options.text: Teamspace archived
options.explanation: Archiving affects the entire teamspace structure, not the removal of a specific member.

options.text: Member teamspace role updated
options.explanation: This event tracks changes to a user's role (e.g., from member to owner), not their removal.

questions.question: What is the retention limit for data when exporting the Audit Log?

options.text: Up to 365 days back until the time of export
options.explanation: The data goes back 365 days up until 2 hours before the time of export.

options.text: No limit; all historical data is available forever
options.explanation: There is a strict limit; data older than 365 days or newer than 2 hours is not included.

options.text: Only data from the last 30 days
options.explanation: The retention window is 365 days, not 30 days.

options.text: Up to 365 days back until 2 hours before the time of export
options.explanation: An exported audit log includes events from 365 days ago up until 2 hours before the export is generated.

questions.question: Which Workspace event type relates to verifying workspace access via SAML Single Sign-On (SSO)?

options.text: Authorized via Workspace SAML
options.explanation: This specific event type verifies that a user accessed the workspace via SAML SSO.

options.text: IdP metadata URL updated
options.explanation: Updating the IdP metadata URL relates to configuring SAML settings, not the act of verifying access.

options.text: SCIM token generated
options.explanation: SCIM tokens are used for provisioning users, not for verifying SSO access.

options.text: MFA TOTP toggled
options.explanation: MFA toggling relates to two-factor authentication, which is a separate security layer from SSO verification.

questions.question: What does the 'Private content transferred' event signify?

options.text: A user moved their private pages to a different workspace
options.explanation: Moving pages is handled by the 'Page moved' event. 'Private content transferred' refers to a specific action regarding a user leaving the workspace.

options.text: Private pages of a user who left the workspace were transferred to a current user
options.explanation: This event occurs when the private pages of a former user are transferred to a current user in the workspace.

options.text: A user exported their private content to a CSV file
options.explanation: Exporting is a separate event type ('Page exported').

options.text: A user deleted their private content from the workspace
options.explanation: Deletion is handled by the 'Page deleted' event.
